tech_stack.frontend: React 19, Vite, Tailwind v4, shadcn/ui, React Router v7, Apollo Client, react-i18next
tech_stack.backend: NestJS 10, Apollo GraphQL, CQRS pattern, Passport JWT
tech_stack.ai: Groq (Whisper transcription), Gemini (AI summary), Cloudinary (audio storage)

flows.name: Log In to the Platform
flows.description: User opens the login page, enters email and password, and is redirected to the dashboard.

steps.expected_state: Login form with email and password fields

error_scenarios.expected_behavior: Error toast displayed, stay on login page

flows.name: Admin Dashboard Overview
flows.description: Admin sees platform statistics: total users by role, post statuses donut chart, attendance line chart over time, recent activity feed, and quick-action buttons.

steps.expected_state: Admin dashboard with stat cards, charts, activity feed

error_scenarios.expected_behavior: Skeleton loaders shown, then empty state or error message

flows.name: Teacher Dashboard Overview
flows.description: Teacher sees group attendance charts, recent activity, and quick-action shortcuts for common tasks.

steps.expected_state: Teacher dashboard with group attendance charts and activity

flows.name: Student Dashboard Overview
flows.description: Student sees their own attendance chart over time and recent activity in the school.

steps.expected_state: Student dashboard showing personal attendance chart

flows.name: Browse Attendance Groups
flows.description: User opens the attendance section and sees all groups they can interact with. Teachers see all groups, students see only their enrolled groups.

steps.expected_state: Grid of group cards, each showing group name and member count

flows.name: Take Attendance for a Group
flows.description: Teacher or admin opens a group's attendance roster, marks each student as present/absent/late/excused, and submits the attendance for the day.

steps.description: Mark each student with a status: present, absent, late, or excused

error_scenarios.expected_behavior: Roster shows existing records, re-submission updates them

flows.name: View Attendance Report
flows.description: View a summary table of attendance records for a group over a selected time period.

steps.description: Open attendance detail page for a group
Table shows each student's attendance percentage and record counts

flows.name: Export Attendance Report
flows.description: Teacher or admin exports attendance data for a group as CSV or PDF.

steps.description: Export job created, download starts or link provided

error_scenarios.trigger: No attendance records in selected period
error_scenarios.expected_behavior: Export generates empty file or warning

flows.name: Student Views Own Attendance
flows.description: Student or teacher views a specific student's individual attendance history and summary statistics.

steps.description: Student attendance summary shown: present/absent/late/excused counts and history records

flows.name: Manage Users (Admin)
flows.description: Admin views all users in a table, creates new users, edits existing ones, deletes users individually or in bulk, and can import users from a CSV file.

steps.description: Users table shows all users with name, email, role columns. Filter and search available.
New user appears in the table

flows.name: Manage Groups (Admin/Teacher)
flows.description: Create, edit, and delete groups. Add or remove members from a group. View group membership details.

steps.description: Switch to Groups tab to see all groups in a grid
Add or remove members from the group

error_scenarios.trigger: Delete group with existing attendance records

flows.name: Import Users from CSV (Admin)
flows.description: Admin uploads a CSV file to bulk-create or update users in the system.

steps.description: Upload a CSV with name, email, password, role columns
Import result shown: N users created, M errors listed

error_scenarios.expected_behavior: Skipped rows listed in error summary

flows.name: Browse Blog Feed
flows.description: User browses published blog posts, filters by category, and sees post cards with title, excerpt, author, and reaction counts.

steps.description: Feed shows published posts as cards. Category filter chips at top.
Feed updates to show posts in selected category

flows.name: Read a Blog Post
flows.description: User opens a blog post to read the full content, see images, react with emojis, and read/write comments and replies.

steps.description: Full post content rendered, images shown, reaction bar visible
Add or remove an emoji reaction to the post
Comment appears in the comment list

error_scenarios.trigger: Post not found (deleted or invalid ID)
error_scenarios.expected_behavior: 404 state or redirect to blog feed
Submit button disabled or validation error

flows.name: Create a Blog Post
flows.description: User writes a new blog post with a title, excerpt, rich content, optional category tags, and optional images. Students submit as draft for review; teachers can publish directly.

steps.target: Publish or Submit for Review button

steps.description: Post created. Teachers/admins see it published; students see it in 'my drafts' pending review.

error_scenarios.expected_behavior: Error toast, post creation can continue without image

flows.name: Review Draft Queue (Admin/Teacher)
flows.description: Teacher or admin reviews blog posts submitted by students, approving or rejecting each one with an optional note.

steps.description: List of student-submitted posts awaiting review
Approve the post — it becomes published

rejectionNote.sample_value: El contenido no cumple con las normas.

steps.description: Post is rejected with a note visible to the author

flows.name: Manage Blog Categories (Admin/Teacher)
flows.description: Create, rename, and delete blog categories used to organize posts.

steps.description: List of existing categories with edit and delete actions

flows.name: Browse and Favorite Tools
flows.description: User opens the tools section, browses tools grouped by category, pins favorites, and navigates to an individual tool.

steps.description: Tool cards grouped by category. Favorite (star) button on each card.

flows.name: Use Countdown Timer
flows.description: Set a countdown timer with hours/minutes/seconds. Start, pause, and reset. Plays an alert when time runs out.

steps.description: Timer counts down in real time

flows.name: Use Quick Notes
flows.description: Write short notes that are saved to the user's account and persist across sessions.

steps.description: Note appears in the notes list

flows.name: Use Task Organizer
flows.description: Create a personal to-do list with tasks that can be checked off, reordered by drag-and-drop, and deleted.

flows.name: Random Student Picker (Teacher)
flows.description: Teacher uses the random student picker to randomly select a student from an enrolled group, useful for classroom participation.

steps.description: Animate and display a random student name

flows.name: Text Simplifier (AI)
flows.description: Paste complex text and the tool simplifies it using AI, making it easier to read and understand.

steps.description: Simplified text appears below the input

flows.name: Screenshot to Text (OCR)
flows.description: Upload an image containing text and extract the text content using OCR (Optical Character Recognition).

steps.description: Upload a screenshot or image with text
Extracted text displayed in a text area for copying

error_scenarios.expected_behavior: Empty extraction result or warning message

flows.name: Use Utility Tools
flows.description: Access and use utility tools: Image Compressor, Image Format Converter, Password Generator, QR Code Generator, Ruleta (Wheel Spinner), Scientific Calculator, Word/PDF Converter, YouTube Downloader.

flows.name: View All Recordings (Admin/Teacher)
flows.description: Teacher or admin sees the list of all their recorded audio sessions with transcription status indicators.

steps.description: Table of recordings with name, duration, date, and transcription status columns

error_scenarios.expected_behavior: Empty state with a button to create first recording

flows.name: Record and Upload Audio (Admin/Teacher)
flows.description: Teacher records audio directly in the browser or uploads an existing audio file. The recording is saved to Cloudinary and a transcription job is triggered.

steps.description: Upload recording to Cloudinary and save to database
Recording saved, transcription status set to 'processing'

error_scenarios.expected_behavior: Error message asking user to grant microphone permission

flows.name: View Transcription Result
flows.description: Teacher opens a completed recording to read the full transcript, listen to the audio, and view or generate an AI summary with key takeaways and action items.

steps.description: Full transcript displayed with timestamps. Audio player controls available.

prompt.sample_value: Resume los puntos clave de esta clase

steps.description: Summary, key takeaways, and action items displayed

error_scenarios.expected_behavior: Processing status shown, transcript not yet available
summaryStatus set to 'failed', error message shown

guards.description: Validates JWT access token. All protected routes require this.
Checks CASL ability from user role against @Can() decorator on resolvers
React component that redirects to /dashboard if ability.can() returns false

limitations.description: Students cannot access /people (user/group management) — requires Action.CREATE on Subject.GROUP
Students cannot access /transcription — requires Action.CREATE on Subject.RECORDING
Only admins can create, update, or delete users
Only admins and teachers can approve/reject blog drafts, manage categories, take attendance, export reports
Students can only SUBMIT posts (creates draft); posts must be approved by teacher/admin to become published
Soft deletes on User, Group, Post, Category, Comment, Tool, Folder, Recording — records are never permanently removed
AI summary generation requires a completed transcription first
Text simplifier and transcription use external AI services (Groq/Gemini) — availability depends on API keys and service uptime
UI is Spanish-first with i18n support for English; all user-facing text goes through react-i18next

glossary_candidates.definition: A class or study group that students belong to and teachers manage for attendance tracking
Attendance — the daily record of whether each student was present, absent, late, or excused
Draft — a blog post that has been written but not yet published; student posts go through a draft/review workflow
Draft queue — the list of student-submitted posts waiting for teacher/admin approval
AI-generated text transcript of an audio recording
AI-generated summary of a transcription including key takeaways and action items
Tools — the suite of productivity utilities available to all users
A tool that a user has bookmarked for quick access
An emoji reaction added to a blog post or comment
